name: Re:amaze Customer Support & Messaging API
what_it_does: Customer support and messaging platform built for e-commerce — with REST API for managing conversations, contacts, and messages across email, live chat, social media, and SMS, with deep Shopify and BigCommerce integration.
use_cases: Managing e-commerce customer support conversations with order context via API
Creating support tickets from order management systems or monitoring tools
Syncing customer contact data between Re:amaze and e-commerce platforms
Building chatbot flows that escalate to human agents via Re:amaze API
Querying conversation history and customer data for analytics agents
not_for: Non-e-commerce support teams without Shopify/BigCommerce context needs
Enterprise ITSM with complex change management workflows
best_when: An e-commerce team needs customer support with native Shopify/BigCommerce order context, live chat, and API access for integration with order management workflows.
avoid_when: You are not an e-commerce business or need enterprise ITSM capabilities.

auth.notes: API token from Re:amaze account settings. HTTP Basic auth with email as username and token as password. Brand-scoped API — specify brand subdomain in endpoint URL. No OAuth2.

pricing.paid_tiers: Basic from $29/agent/month; Pro from $49; Plus from $69; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Per-agent pricing. Acquired by GoDaddy in 2021 — stability concerns for some. Strong e-commerce focus with Shopify/BigCommerce native integrations. Good for DTC brands.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply — documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers conversations, messages, contacts, and reports. Webhook support for conversation events. Brand subdomain required in API URL — multi-brand agents must parameterize. HTTP Basic auth with email/token.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Conversation creation creates new items. Contact upsert available. Message sends are not idempotent. Read operations always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: GoDaddy acquisition — product roadmap less transparent; verify continued development
Brand subdomain required in all API endpoints — agents must track brand context
HTTP Basic auth requires email as username — not just token
E-commerce context requires Shopify/BigCommerce connection — agents can query order data once connected

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth. GDPR compliant. GoDaddy parent company security practices apply. US data residency. Webhook signature verification for event delivery.
